verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN51) encodes a type I integral membrane protein that cycles constitutively between the trans-Golgi network and early endosomes. Its steady-state residence is overwhelmingly in the TGN lumen-facing membrane, not the plasma membrane. Although it briefly transits the plasma membrane as part of its endocytic recycling itinerary, this is a very transient passage and the protein is not detectably concentrated at the cell surface under baseline conditions — it is the canonical TGN marker. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer ecto-pool or cell-death-induced PM display; no germline/developmental-restricted surface expression; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing exocytosis; no stable minority PM pool reported by surface proteomics or flow cytometry; no TM-partner tethering story. No clinical surface-directed therapeutic program targets it. The endomembrane_resident call dominates.
